DISCOVERY 302, (PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON MAY 24,2022.) PETITIONER JOHN DOE'S PETITION TO QUASH SUBPOENA PURSUANT TO CAL. CIV. PROC. SECS. 1987.1(A) AND 2029.600 IS GRANTED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (302)
DECLARATION OF ETHAN JACOBS IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO QUASH SUBPOENA (TRANSACTION ID # 100162209) FILED BY PETITIONER DOE, JOHN

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, MAY-24-2022 HEARING HELD RE: PETITIONER JOHN DOE'S PETITION TO QUASH SUBPOENA PURSUANT TO CAL. CIV. PROC. SECS. 1987.1(A) AND 2029.600. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON THE MERITS AND TAKES UNDER SUBMISSION THE ISSUE OF FEES. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER ON A POSSIBLE AGREEMENT AS TO THE FEES ISSUE AND EMAIL THE PRO TEM JUDGE AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM IF THEY ARE ABLE TO REACH AN AGREEMENT. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Of Petition To Quash Subpoena Pursuant To Cal. Civ. Proc. 1987.1(A) And 2029.600
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ETHAN JACOBS, Esq. of Ethan Jacobs Law Corporation (Tel: 415-275-0845) for petitioner John Doe;
Atty. RUBEN PENA, Esq. of Kronenberger Rosenfeld, LLP (Tel: 415-955-1155) for respondents Incongruity, LLC and Mike Boudet.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER JOHN DOE's Petition To Quash Subpoena Pursuant To Cal. Civ. Proc. Secs. 1987.1(A) And 2029.600.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling on the merits and takes under submission the issue of fees. Parties are to meet and confer on a possible agreement as to the fees issue and email the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com if they are able to reach an agreement.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
